explicit_text: My name is Donald Adolph Adam, born in Waukesha, Wisconsin, into a family deeply rooted in the brewing industry. I grew up working in the Fox Head Brewery, learning the craft as part of a German five-year apprenticeship tradition. However, my passion shifted to flying after a memorable airplane ride in high school, which led me to earn my pilot's license before joining the Army Air Corps in 1945 during World War II. I later trained on various aircraft and served actively, balancing my early life in brewing with my military aviation career.

explicit_text: I enlisted during World War II, specifically in 1944 while I was still in high school, and was called to active duty on my 18th birthday in 1945. The war created a huge demand for pilots, which motivated me to pursue flying lessons and join the Army Air Corps. My service during this time was shaped by the urgent need for trained pilots to support the war effort.
